transcript.text: Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a minute. What've you got for me today?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Here's the thing though — heads up on this one. We've got an odometer variance. The bid came in at fifty-nine eight, but Carfax is showing fifty-five two-eighty-three. I wanted to be straight with you first.

Otherwise it's clean — no accidents, no damage. Title's clear. And the condition inside and out is solid. We're at thirty-one five on this one, Sarah.

What's your thinking?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, I appreciate you being straight with me on that variance — that's good. But Bill, you gotta understand, that kind of discrepancy is a red flag for my buyers. Even if it's clean otherwise, I'm gonna have to price it accordingly.

Thirty-one five? That's high for a unit with odometer questions hanging over it. 

What's your bottom line here? I'm interested, but I need you to come down. I'm thinking more in the twenty-seven, twenty-eight K range given what we're working with. That gives me some room to move it and covers my exposure on the variance issue.

What can you do?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I hear you — and that's fair thinking. The variance is real, and it's a conversation point on the lot. But here's what I'm looking at: rBook on this car is forty-two eight, and we're already nine grand under that. MMR's at twenty-seven, so we're about four-five above market comp.

The odometer thing — it's flagged, buyers will know it upfront, but the car itself is clean. No accident history, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. That spec doesn't change because of the mileage question.

I can move thirty-one five down to thirty point five. But I need something in return — faster settlement, or you lock in the next premium unit that comes through. What does that get me?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright, Bill, I hear you on the specs and the book value — that's solid intel. And I appreciate you coming down to thirty point five. That's movement.

But here's my reality: that odometer variance is still my problem to solve on the lot. Yeah, the car's clean, but I'm the one fielding questions from buyers who see that flag. That's friction I gotta manage.

I can do twenty-nine K. That's real money off your ask, and it puts me in a position where I can actually move this thing without it sitting.

Now — on your ask for something in return? I can work with that. I'll lock in the next premium unit you've got coming through. You get priority placement with me on your next clean wholesale piece. That's good for your pipeline, and it shows good faith.

But I need you at twenty-nine. That's where I can commit. Can you get there?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the commitment on the next unit — that's real. And I get the friction on the variance, I do.

But twenty-nine's too steep. We're already holding the bag on some of the risk here by pricing it this way. I can do twenty-nine five, and that locks in your priority on the next premium piece. That's a thousand-dollar move from thirty point five, and you get first look at whatever comes in clean.

Twenty-nine five gets you in the game and protects your margin. Can we land there?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pauses*

You know what, Bill? I respect the negotiation here. You came down a grand, and you're putting real value on the table with that priority placement. That matters.

Twenty-nine five works. I can do that.

Lock me in on the next premium unit — I want first look, no exceptions. And I'll need the settlement to move faster than standard, yeah? I'm committing capital here with the variance hanging over it.

Get me the paperwork and let's move this. And Bill — when that next clean piece comes in, I expect you to remember this conversation.

We good?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Yeah, send it.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Perfect. I'll get the docs over to you by end of day. My guy will handle settlement — let's shoot for three business days if you can swing it.

And Bill, I meant what I said about the next one. You take care of me, I take care of you. That's how this works.

Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk soon, Sarah. You got it.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a minute. What've you got for me today? 

You know I'm always looking, but my cash is tight right now so we're gonna need to make the numbers work. What are we talking about?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Right, good to hear. So I got a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i that just came in. Sixty thousand miles, loaded — Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, the whole nine yards. Black Sapphire.

Here's the thing though — there's an odometer variance on this one. Carfax shows fifty-five thousand, bid came in at sixty. Wanted to lead with that because it's the headline. Clean otherwise, no damage flags.

Where we're at on it: thirty-one five. And here's why that number makes sense for you — rBook comps are sitting at forty, so you're looking at near nine grand spread right there. Wholesale market's soft on these anyway, so this gives you room to move it.

Could this pencil for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, Bill, I appreciate you leading with the odometer thing — that's real. That's the kind of transparency that keeps us doing business.

But here's where I'm at: that variance is a problem. Yeah, it's clean otherwise, but now I gotta disclose that, and it's gonna spook some buyers. You know how it is. That's friction on my end.

And look — thirty-one five is high for what I'm looking at. I get that rBook says forty, but you said it yourself: the market's soft on these. I'm not paying book in a soft market. I need margin to breathe.

Here's what I can do: **twenty-eight K**. That gives me room to work with the variance issue, and honestly, that's where I gotta be on this one.

What's your bottom line? Can you move on price, or do you have room to sweeten this some other way — maybe faster delivery, or we talk about future credit on the next deal?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you on the variance — that's fair. And you're right, the market's soft. I'm not gonna argue with that.

But twenty-eight is too far down. That puts us underwater on this one. Here's the reality: MMR's at twenty-seven, so at twenty-eight you're literally buying at wholesale plus a hundred bucks. That doesn't work for us, and honestly, it won't work for you either because we can't do the deal.

Let me come back at you with thirty K. That's five-fifty off my ask, gives you a little room on the variance disclosure, and you're still picking up solid rBook comps.

And here's what I can add to make that sweeten: you take it today, we can have it on your lot by tomorrow morning. No waiting around.

What do you think on thirty flat?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pauses*

Okay, Bill — I appreciate the MMR reality check. You're not wrong there. And tomorrow delivery? That actually matters to me. You know I move these fast, and having it on the lot by morning helps.

But thirty is still a stretch. I'm looking at this thing and I've got that odometer variance hanging over it. That's real friction when I'm retailing it. It's not like you're handing me a clean title story — I'm explaining a Carfax discrepancy to every buyer who walks around it.

Here's where I land: **twenty-nine K**. That's a solid move for you off the ask, it gives me breathing room on the variance, and with the next-day delivery, that sweetens the deal enough for me to say yes today.

Twenty-nine and it's done. Can you get there?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, you're negotiating smart — I'll give you that. And I get the variance friction, I do.

But here's where I'm stuck: twenty-nine still leaves us too thin. You're asking me to move another thousand off thirty, and at that point the math just doesn't pencil for us on intake.

Here's what I can do though: twenty-nine-five. That splits the difference, gets you closer to where you need to be on the variance story, and with next-day delivery locked in, you've got real margin to work with.

That's my floor on this one, Sarah. Twenty-nine-five, and I'll make sure it's on your lot by EOD tomorrow.

Does that work?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*takes a beat*

You know what, Bill? Twenty-nine-five and next-day delivery... yeah, that works for me. 

I wanted twenty-eight, you needed thirty-one, and we landed in a place that makes sense for both of us. That's how this is supposed to work.

Get me the paperwork and let's lock this in. I want that BMW on my lot EOD tomorrow like you said. And Bill — I'm gonna need you to flag that variance clear on the title docs so there's no surprises on my end when I'm retailing it. Deal?

Let's do this.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect, Sarah. I appreciate you meeting me in the middle — that's exactly how it should work.

So twenty-nine-five, locked in. Next-day delivery, I'm on it. And I'll make sure the variance is flagged crystal clear on all the paperwork before it rolls out — no surprises when you're selling it.

While we're on the phone, can I text you the bid card with all the details? That way you've got it in writing, and one of our owners will follow up in the next couple hours to finalize the paperwork and confirm the pickup time for tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Yeah, absolutely. Send it over. I want everything in writing anyway — you know how I operate.

And Bill? Make sure that follow-up happens. I need the pickup time locked in solid. I'm counting on EOD tomorrow, so don't leave me hanging on logistics.

Text me the bid card and have your owner call me within the next two hours. I'll have my team ready to receive it.

Good doing business with you on this one.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Done. Sending it right now — you should see it in the next minute or two.

And I'm locking in the owner callback within two hours. They'll have the pickup details locked down solid, and that BMW rolls out EOD tomorrow like we said. You'll have your team ready and we'll have it there.

Good doing business with you too, Sarah. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Yeah, I've got a few minutes. What's going on? You got something for me?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. Here's the thing though — we've got an odometer variance flagged on the Carfax. Fifty-nine eight on the bid, fifty-five two on the history. I wanted to lead with that because it's the headline. Otherwise clean title, sharp car. We're looking at thirty-one five.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, okay — I appreciate you leading with that. That's the kind of transparency I need to hear. But look, that odometer variance is a real problem for me. Even if it clears up, it's gonna spook my buyers. That's friction I gotta work through.

So here's where I'm at: the car itself, loaded like that? I'd normally be interested. But with that flag hanging over it, I can do **twenty-eight K**. That gives me some breathing room to deal with the title issue and still move it.

That's a solid thirty percent off your ask. What's your bottom line here, Bill?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you — that's a real concern, and I'm not gonna pretend the variance doesn't create work on your end. But twenty-eight is too steep. Here's my thinking: that odometer mismatch is explainable. It happens. Carfax gets re-reported, title clears. The car itself is fifty-nine K miles, pampered history, Executive loaded. That's the real asset.

What if we land at thirty flat? That gives you a grand and a half of cushion to handle the admin, but I'm still holding value on what this car actually is underneath that flag.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Look, I hear you on the car being solid underneath — and I'm not saying it's not. But Bill, you're asking me to take on the risk and the headache while you keep most of the upside. That doesn't work for me.

Thirty is still high. The variance is real friction, and even if it clears, I'm the one holding the bag until it does. My buyer walks if there's any doubt.

Here's where I want to be: **twenty-nine K**. That's a meaningful move from me, but it gets you closer to your number and gives me enough cushion to handle the title work and actually have confidence selling this thing.

And look — if you can sweeten the deal somehow, we might have something. Can you do faster delivery? Or maybe throw in some future credit on the next deal? That helps me justify moving up a notch.

What do you think?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You're making a fair point on the risk side — I'll give you that. Twenty-nine is closer, but I'm still short of where I need to be on this one. Here's the thing though: that variance is gonna clear. We've seen it a hundred times. Carfax gets updated, title paperwork resolves, and your buyer never even knows it was a question. You're pricing in worst-case scenario.

But I get what you're saying about needing breathing room. Here's what I can do: thirty flat, and I'll push this through as a priority settlement. You can have it prepped and on your lot within five days. That gives you a clean window to get the title sorted before your customer even shows up.

And on the next solid unit that comes through — something in your wheelhouse — you get first dialing. That's real value.

Can we land on thirty with those terms?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pauses*

Okay, Bill. Five days is actually helpful — I'm not gonna lie. And first dialing on your next solid unit? That matters to me.

But here's the thing — you're still asking me to bet that variance clears smoothly. Even with five days, I'm taking on that execution risk. What if Carfax doesn't update when you think it will? What if the title paperwork gets hung up? Now I'm scrambling.

So I need a little more cushion. I can do **twenty-nine-five** with those terms — the five-day priority push and first look at your next solid car. That's me meeting you in the middle. I get a real buffer for worst-case scenario, you get the faster turn and we lock in future business.

That work for you?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You're playing this right, Sarah. I respect that. Twenty-nine-five is tight, but you've earned it with real concessions — you're taking the variance risk, you're committed to moving it fast with the five-day window, and we're building a relationship on the next one.

Let me lock this in with the owners real quick so I'm not overstating my authority on the settlement timeline and that first-look commitment. That stuff needs to come from them. Sound good?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Yeah, absolutely — I respect that. You gotta make sure your people are locked in on those terms. I'm not trying to shake hands on something that falls apart on your end.

Go ahead, touch base with them. But Bill, when you come back, I need those commitments in writing — the five-day settlement and that first-look deal. No ambiguity. That's what makes this work for me.

And look, if they push back on twenty-nine-five, we can talk. But those concessions are what got us there, so if they're off the table, the number moves too. Fair?

Let me know what they say.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk to you in ten, Sarah.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Sounds good, Bill. I'll be here. Talk in ten.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk to you in ten.

transcript.text: You got it. I'll be waiting for your call.

transcript.text: Yep, no problem. Talk soon, Bill.

transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, loaded with the Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro — black sapphire, really clean. Here's the thing though — there's an odometer variance we flagged on intake. Carfax shows fifty-five thousand two hundred eighty-three, but the actual mileage is fifty-nine eight. Wanted to lead with that because transparency matters.

Beyond that, car runs solid. MMR's sitting around twenty-seven, rBook's forty. We're targeting thirty-one five on it. Could this work for your lot?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, I appreciate the transparency on the mileage discrepancy — that's actually refreshing. But let's talk about what that does to my position here.

So you've got a title issue — odometer variance is a red flag for my customers, even if the car runs clean. That's going to sit longer on my lot, and I'm gonna have to disclose it, which means I'm moving it at a discount anyway. You're asking thirty-one-five, but you know as well as I do that gap between Carfax and actual mileage is a liability.

Look, I want this car. The Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, that's good stuff — those cars move. But I gotta account for the disclosure hit and the slower turn. 

I can do twenty-eight. That gives me room to move it properly and still make sense of the odometer thing.

What's your bottom line here, Bill? And be straight with me — are you flexible, or is thirty-one-five locked in?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you, Sarah — that's a fair read on the disclosure hit and turn time. You're thinking like a real operator. Let me be straight: thirty-one-five is not locked, but it's not twenty-eight either.

Here's my thinking. The odometer variance is real, but it's not a title brand or frame damage — it's a disclosure that gets noted and priced. What actually moves this car is the spec. Executive Package, Driving Assist Pro, sixty K miles on a seven-fifty — that's a solid rental-return or one-owner private buy that your floor can turn. The variance costs you maybe five to seven hundred on the backend, not a grand-plus.

I'll move to thirty. That's a real flex from my number, and it gives you room to work it. But I need something from you too — can you turn it in thirty days? If you can lock it down that fast, thirty works. If it's gonna sit, we need to talk about it differently.

What's your realistic turn window on this one?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright, Bill — I respect that you're actually listening and moving the needle. Thirty's better. And you're right that the variance isn't a title issue, just a disclosure headwind.

Here's my honest turn window: thirty days is aggressive on a thirty-one-five ask, even with the spec. Maybe twenty-eight to thirty-five depending on how I price it and what traffic looks like. Could be faster, could be slower. I can't guarantee thirty locked.

But here's what I *can* do — I'll commit to putting it on the front line, front-page placement for the first thirty days. That means real visibility. That's where your car gets sold. And if it moves in thirty days, I'm not coming back asking for a restock discount later.

So I'll split the difference with you: twenty-nine-five. That gives me a working margin, and you get the aggressive placement commitment plus my word that this thing gets prioritized. You get paid faster, I get a realistic margin to work with.

That work for you, or do we need to keep negotiating?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I like how you're thinking about this — front-line placement for thirty days is real value, and I trust your word on it. That's the kind of commitment that actually moves cars.

Twenty-nine-five is closer, but it's tight for me. Here's where I land: thirty. And here's what I need in exchange — not just the placement, but a non-refundable deposit today to lock it in. That shows me you're serious, and it gives me the security I need to move off thirty-one-five down to thirty.

How much deposit are we talking — a grand, twenty-five hundred?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, I see what you're doing here, Bill — and I get it. You want skin in the game to lock in the move from thirty-one-five to thirty.

Here's the thing though: a deposit on a car I haven't even physically inspected yet? That's not how I operate. I don't do non-refundable deposits before my own walk-through. I need to see this thing in person — check the interior, run my own diagnostic, make sure the odometer variance doesn't have any other flags attached.

That said, I'm not walking away. I like this car, and I like your word on the front-end work.

Here's what I *will* do: I'll commit to coming see it this week — let's say by Friday. Once I walk it and it checks out the way you're saying, I'll lock it in at thirty with a refundable deposit to hold it while I arrange transport and paperwork. That's real commitment without me betting blind.

Can you have it ready for a Friday inspection? And if it's clean like you say, we've got a deal at thirty.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Got it sent. You'll see the full card — Carfax, damage audit, all the options breakdown. Take a look at it before Friday if you get time.

So we're locked at thirty, you inspect Friday, and if it's clean like we've talked through, we've got a deal. You'll bring a deposit to hold it while you handle transport and paperwork — makes sense?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Perfect. I'm gonna grab that card right now and give it a look tonight.

And yeah — to be crystal clear on what we're locking in: I inspect Friday, car checks out like you've represented it, we're at thirty flat. I'll bring a refundable deposit to hold it, we sort transport and paperwork after. That's the deal.

One thing though — if I walk it Friday and something doesn't line up with what's on that card, we're reopening the conversation. I'm not locked in blind. But if it's clean, we're good.

Send me the location and a time that works best for Friday. And Bill? I appreciate you being straight about the odometer thing from the jump. That's how you do business.

Talk Friday.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Appreciate that, Sarah. You got it — if something doesn't line up Friday, we talk about it then. Fair is fair.

I'm gonna have one of the owners send you the location and confirm a Friday time slot. They'll reach out within the hour so you can nail down when works best for you.

And thanks for the trust. Talk Friday.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Sounds good. I'll keep an eye out for their message — get me a time and I'll make it work.

One last thing, Bill — make sure that car's detailed and ready to roll when I get there. I want to see it clean so I can actually evaluate what I'm looking at. No surprises.

Talk to you Friday.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: You got it — car'll be spotless and ready for you. See you Friday, Sarah.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Perfect. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Take care.

Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a minute. What've you got for me today? You know I'm always looking, but you also know how this works — I'm gonna need the right price.

Clean Carfax. One flag — odometer variance. We're seeing fifty-nine eight on the bid, Carfax is showing fifty-five two-eighty-three. That's the disclosure.

Could this work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, okay — I hear you. That's a nice spec'd out seven-fifty. The packages are solid, and yeah, clean Carfax is good. But let's talk about that elephant in the room first.

That odometer variance — we're talking four-and-a-half *thousand* miles unaccounted for. That's a red flag Bill, and you know it. That's gonna haunt me on the back end. My buyers are gonna see that disclosure and immediately think "what else don't I know?" It tanks confidence, it tanks resale.

So here's where I'm at: MMR's twenty-seven, you're asking thirty-one five. That's a gap. And with that variance hanging over it, I can't touch thirty-one five. 

I can do **twenty-eight K**. That gives me room to work with the odometer issue and still make sense of the numbers.

What's your bottom line here, Bill? And be straight with me — is there any flex on your end, or are we just spinning our wheels?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you. Four-and-a-half K is material, and you're right — your buyers are gonna ask hard questions about it. That's fair pushback.

Here's how I see it though: yeah, there's a variance, but both Carfax and AutoCheck came back clean otherwise. No accident history, no title issues, service records are there. On the spread: twenty-eight is tight. That puts us right at MMR with no margin for you, and we've got real money in this car. rBook's at forty-two. You're looking at nine, ten grand spread to retail even with the flag.

Here's where I can move: I can do thirty flat. That gives you three grand of cushion to work the odometer angle with your customers, and you're still underneath rBook by ten grand. That's real margin.

Here's what I'm thinking: I can get to **twenty-nine two**. That's real movement from me. That gives you some cushion, gives me room to work the car without bleeding out.

Twenty-nine three and Oscar handles your transport personally. That's the move.

transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a few minutes. What's going on? You got something for me?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i — sixty thousand miles, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. Black Sapphire, really clean unit.

Here's where I'm at: MMR's around twenty-seven, rBook's forty, and I'm targeting thirty-one five on this one. Could be a strong fit for you.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Thirty-one five? Bill, come on. I appreciate the call, but that's high and you know it. 

Look, I like the unit — low miles, loaded out, clean paint. But MMR's sitting at twenty-seven. I'm not gonna pay a five grand spread over market on a seven-fifty. That's a tough sell on my lot.

What's your bottom line here? I want it, but you gotta come down. I can do twenty-eight K, maybe twenty-eight-five if it's as clean as you're saying. But thirty-one is just not happening.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you — that's fair math on your side. But let me walk you through this: rBook's at forty. We're nine grand under book. That's not a five K spread over MMR — that's us giving up real money on the rBook side to move it fast.

Twenty-eight to twenty-eight-five puts us upside down on cost. I can't get there.

Here's what I can do: thirty K flat. Condition's solid — both Carfax and AutoCheck are clean, and I want to mention upfront there is an odometer variance we caught on intake. Bid shows fifty-nine-eight, Carfax shows fifty-five-two. Disclosed it already, it's on the report.

At thirty K, you're still five grand under rBook and you can move it. What do you think — can we make that work?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, hold up — I appreciate you walking me through the math, but now you're dropping an odometer variance on me? That's a wrinkle I need to think about. That changes things.

Look, I get that you're between a rock and a hard place on cost, but thirty K is still steep for me, especially with that mileage discrepancy in play. That's going to affect my retail story, whether it's disclosed or not. Buyers see that flag, they're gonna negotiate me harder on the backend.

Here's where I'm at: I can do twenty-nine K on this thing. That gets you closer to your number, but it accounts for the odometer variance I'm gonna have to work around.

But Bill — if that's still not workable, what else you got for me? Can you throw in some dealer credit for future buys? Or maybe accelerate delivery on another unit I've been looking at? Give me something to work with here and we might be able to close this out.

What do you say?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I get it — that variance is a real retail headwind for you, and I should've led with that instead of burying it. That's on me.

Twenty-nine is closer, but honestly it's still light. Here's what I can offer you: twenty-nine-five. That's a half K move for me, and it puts you at a number where you can retail it strong even with the disclosure sitting there.

On the dealer credit side — I can't commit to that solo, Oscar would need to lock that in. But if you want to do twenty-nine-five on this unit, I'll have him reach out within the hour and we can talk future inventory priority and terms that actually work for your cashflow. Fair?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Twenty-nine-five... and Oscar's gonna call me about priority inventory and cashflow terms.

Alright, Bill. I like where your head's at on this — you're being straight with me about the variance, you moved a half K, and you're putting some real skin in the game with Oscar on the backend. That matters.

Yeah, I can do twenty-nine-five on this one. But I'm gonna hold you to that — Oscar needs to call me within the hour. I'm not messing around. And when he does, we're talking front-of-line access on your next couple incoming, not someday down the road.

Get me the clean title docs and the full Carfax report with that variance clearly marked, and we're good. I'll have my guy pick it up early next week if that works for you.

Deal?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Deal. I'm sending you the bid card right now with everything — title docs, full Carfax, variance flagged clearly. You'll see it in a second.

Oscar's gonna call you within the hour. He's gonna make sure you've got front-of-line access on the next couple solid units that hit us. I'm not gonna BS you — he means it.

Your guy can grab it early next week, that works perfect for us. Sarah, good talking to you. You held your line and I respect that.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Appreciate that, Bill. And I respect you too — you came straight with me on that variance instead of hiding it. That goes a long way.

Got the bid card, I'll have my team review everything tonight. Make sure that title's clean and we're locked in.

Oscar better actually call me though — *laughs* — I'm not joking. Within the hour, seriously.

My guy will reach out Monday to coordinate pickup. Early week works great for us.

Good doing business with you. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk soon, Sarah. Catch you Monday.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a minute. What've you got for me today? 

*leans back in chair*

Please tell me you've got something good because I'm looking to move some inventory this week.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect timing. Just landed a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Sixty thousand miles, Black Sapphire — fully loaded with the Driving Assistance Pro and Executive Package. Here's the headline: we're at thirty-one five, and that's nearly nine grand under rBook on this one.

Only thing worth flagging up front — odometer mismatch between our intake and Carfax. We caught it, noted it, clean title otherwise. But I wanted to bring it to you anyway because the spread is real and the car is solid.

What do you think — could that work for your lot?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pauses*

Okay, Bill, I appreciate the transparency on the odometer thing — that's good. But let's be real, that's a red flag I gotta disclose, and that cuts into my margins on the back end. Buyers are gonna ask questions.

Thirty-one five is your ask, right? 

Look, the car itself sounds clean and the mileage is reasonable for a seven-fifty. But that title issue — even if it's noted — I gotta price around it. I can do **twenty-seven-five**. That gives me room to move it without getting stuck.

*leans forward*

That's real money off your ask. What's your bottom line here? And more importantly — how solid is the documentation on that odometer discrepancy? Because if it's gonna be a hassle to move, I need better numbers.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I hear you on the disclosure piece — that's fair. Let me be straight with you though: twenty-seven-five puts us below MMR. We're already taking risk on a soft market here, and that number doesn't work for us.

Here's what I'm thinking — the odometer flag is clean. We logged it on intake, it's in the Carfax. No title brand, no mystery. When your customer sees the notation, they know what they're getting. It's not a hidden surprise. That matters.

I can move to thirty, that gets you five hundred off the ask. That's real room for you to work with on the back end without me leaving money on the table. And honestly, at that price with the Executive Package and the condition we've got — you're still nine grand under retail comps. That car will move.

What's driving the twenty-seven-five number for you? Is it the odo flag alone, or are you seeing something else in the market on these that I should know about?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*sits back, thinking*

Alright, I respect that you came down and you're being straight with me on the odometer situation. And yeah, you're right — if it's cleanly documented in Carfax, that's different than a buried surprise. That does matter.

But thirty is still high for me, Bill. I gotta be honest — the odo flag is part of it, but it's not just that. The seven-fifty market's been soft. These are sitting longer than they used to. I've got two on my lot right now, both clean titles, both moving slower than I'd like. So when I'm pricing, I'm pricing for velocity, not just margin.

Here's where I'm at: I can do **twenty-eight-five**. That's a real move from my opening number, and it gets you closer to thirty without me overextending.

*leans forward*

But here's what could move this needle further — what if you throw in a faster delivery window? Or future credit? Like, I buy this one at twenty-eight-five, and you comp me something on my next two units? That works for me because I'm not just eating the price — I'm getting value somewhere else.

What flexibility do you have on your end?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I appreciate that, Sarah — and you're right, the market's been softer than it was six months ago. I get the velocity play.

Twenty-eight-five is closer. Here's where I'm at: I can meet you at twenty-nine flat. That's a real split between thirty and twenty-eight-five, and it gets us both in the middle.

On the delivery and future credit — I like where your head's at, but that's the kind of thing Oscar needs to lock in because it affects the whole relationship, not just this one unit. I don't want to promise you something and then have him come back with a different number. That kills credibility.

What I can do right now is get you the car fast — we can have it ready for pickup or transport by end of week if you move on it. And if you're serious about the future deals, I'll loop Oscar in and you two can work out the structure. Could be volume discount, could be terms, could be both.

So here's my real ask: twenty-nine on this 750i, fast delivery locked in, and I'll have Oscar call you today to talk about what we can

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*nods slowly*

Okay, I like that you're being real about Oscar needing to lock in the bigger stuff. That's smart — no point in me chasing promises that don't stick.

Twenty-nine flat... 

*pauses, taps pen on desk*

Look, that's closer, but I'm still a couple grand north of where I need to be to feel good about this. You came down a point, I came up a point — we're meeting in the middle, but that middle doesn't quite work for my spreadsheet on a car with the odo flag.

Here's what I'll do: I'll take it at **twenty-eight-seven-five** and you lock in that end-of-week delivery. That's real money off your thirty-one-five, and I get the car in my lot fast so I can start turning it.

And yeah, have Oscar call me today. I'm interested in talking about future structure — if the numbers are right, I can be a steady outlet for you guys. But that conversation needs to happen so I know what I'm working with.

*sits back*

Can you do twenty-eight-seven-five with the fast delivery and Oscar on the phone today?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you — and I respect the play here. But twenty-eight-seven-five is below our floor on this one. I've already moved a grand and a half from the ask, and that's where the math runs out for us.

Here's what I can do: twenty-nine stays on the table with fast delivery locked. That's my floor, and it's firm. What I'm gonna do right now is text you the full bid card so you can see everything — the condition report, the options breakdown, the Carfax notation on the odo thing. Look it over and sit with it.

And yeah, Oscar's calling you today. I'm gonna get him on it right after we hang up so he can talk future deals, terms, whatever makes sense for a steady partnership. That conversation might open doors on pricing down the line too — depends on what he can commit to on volume.

But on this unit, at this price: twenty-nine with end-of-week delivery. Does that work, or do you want to think about it?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*leans back, quiet for a moment*

Alright, Bill. I hear you. You've come down solid, you're locking in delivery, and Oscar's calling today — that matters.

Twenty-nine is firm on your end. I get it.

*taps desk*

You know what? I'll take it. But I'm taking it because the car sounds solid, the documentation is clean on the odo thing, and more importantly, I'm betting on Oscar being able to work something out that makes this a better deal long-term. That's the only way this pencils for me.

So yeah — **twenty-nine flat, locked delivery end of week, and Oscar calls me today**. We got a deal.

*extends hand*

Get me that bid card so I can see the full breakdown, and tell Oscar to call before close of business. If we're gonna do volume, I want to know what that looks like.

And Bill? I appreciate you being straight with me from the jump. The odometer thing could've been buried. That goes a long way.

When can you have the paperwork ready?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate that — and you're doing the right thing by making this about the long game, not just this one unit. That's how real partnerships work.

Bid card is going to your phone right now. You'll see the full breakdown, all the options, the Carfax notes on the odo flag — everything clean.

Paperwork will be ready by tomorrow morning. We can have the car prepped for pickup or transport by Thursday or Friday, whichever works better for you. Just let me know your preference once you and Oscar talk.

And yeah, Oscar's gonna call you before five today. He's gonna lock in what a steady flow looks like for you — whether that's pricing, terms, priority on certain vehicles, whatever makes sense.

Good talking to you, Sarah. You're gonna be happy with this one.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*nods, satisfied*

Good. I'll look over that bid card and get back to you on pickup versus transport — probably transport, honestly. Faster that way.

And Bill, one more thing — when Oscar calls, make sure he knows I'm serious about volume. If the numbers work and you guys can prioritize decent inventory for me, I can be a regular customer. But it's gotta be consistent. No cherry-picking on your end, no surprises on condition or pricing. We do it clean, we both win.

*stands up*

Thursday delivery works better for me than Friday, so let's lock that in. I'll have the wire ready once I sign off on the paperwork tomorrow.

Thanks for bringing this to me straight. That's refreshing. Talk to you soon.

*shakes hand firmly*

And make sure Oscar calls before five. I'm serious about that.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You got it, Sarah. Thursday delivery, transport locked in. I'm gonna flag Oscar right now — he'll have all of this in front of him before he calls. And he'll know you're serious about volume and consistency. That's exactly the conversation he wants to have.

Clean, no surprises, regular flow. That's how we work best anyway.

Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill, yeah I got a minute. What's going on? You got something for me?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i — fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, Black Sapphire, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. Here's the thing though — we've got an odometer variance flagged. Carfax shows fifty-five thousand two hundred eighty-three, so there's about a four-thousand-five-hundred-mile discrepancy we caught on intake.

Beyond that, it's clean. We're sitting at thirty-one five on it. Could this work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, hold on — an odometer variance? That's a problem, Bill. That's a *red flag*. Even if it's clean otherwise, I gotta disclose that to my buyers, and it kills the resale story on a car like this. That's not a minor thing.

Thirty-one five is already aggressive for a seven-fifty with that kind of issue hanging over it. I want it, but you gotta come down. What's your bottom line here? I'm thinking low to mid-twenty-eights. Maybe twenty-eight-five if the paperwork's airtight and there's documentation on what caused the variance.

What are we really working with?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I hear you — that's fair. The variance is the headline, and you're right that it affects the story. Let me be straight with you though: twenty-eight-five is too far south. We're already nine grand under rBook at thirty-one five, and the MMR's at twenty-seven, so we're not that far off market.

Here's what I can do — I can move to thirty K flat. That gives you some room to work with your buyer, and you're still in the spread. On the paperwork side, we flagged it on intake and it's documented — no hidden surprises when your customer goes to register it. That's protection for both of us.

What's your gut — does thirty K work, or are we still too far apart?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Thirty K is better, Bill, but we're still not there. I appreciate the transparency on the paperwork — that actually matters to me — but that odometer thing is still a discount driver. Yeah, you're under book, but *I* gotta move it with that liability attached.

Here's where my head's at: I can do twenty-nine K. That's real money off your ask, and it gives me actual margin to work a buyer who knows about the variance.

But look — if you won't budge on price, what else you got? Can you do faster delivery? I got a buyer I'm trying to move something for next week. Or — and this might work — can we talk about future credit? You throw me something on the next deal, we make this work at twenty-nine-five.

What's flexible for you here?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Look, I appreciate you laying it out. Twenty-nine K is tight, but let me see what I can actually move on here without stepping on the owners.

On delivery — I can push fast, probably get it to you by Wednesday if that helps with your buyer timeline. And future credit, I want to make that work too, but that's more of an Oscar conversation since it's a relationship play. I don't want to promise you something I can't lock in.

Here's what I can do right now: twenty-nine-two-five. That's a real move from thirty K, and it gets you closer to where you need to be on margin. You get the faster delivery, and I'll loop Oscar in on the future deal component so that's solid when you're ready to talk next unit. That way nothing's handshake stuff — it's locked.

Does twenty-nine-two-five plus the Wednesday delivery work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pause*

Okay, I like that you're moving and being straight about what you can actually commit to. Wednesday delivery helps — that timing actually works with my buyer situation.

Twenty-nine-two-five... that's closer. You're at least meeting me halfway here instead of digging in.

Here's the thing though — I need twenty-nine K flat to make the numbers work on my end. That's the margin I need to present this to my buyer without feeling like I'm leaving money on the table. But if you lock in the Wednesday delivery *and* you get Oscar to confirm future credit on the next deal we do together, we got a handshake.

Can you commit to both of those right now? Wednesday delivery confirmed, and Oscar gets a heads-up from you today so when I call him, it's already warm?

If that's a yes, I'll do twenty-nine K and we move the car.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You got it. Twenty-nine K, Wednesday delivery locked in, and I'm putting Oscar on notice today that you're calling him about future credit on the next one. That's a handshake deal.

Let me send you the bid card right now so you've got all the details — title, options, the odometer flag, everything. Once you see it, we're golden and you can coordinate with Oscar on the backend.

Hang tight one second.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Perfect. Yeah, send it over — I want eyes on everything before we're officially locked in. Just want to make sure the paperwork's clean on that variance like you said.

And Bill — I appreciate you working with me here. You didn't have to move, but you did, and you brought solutions instead of just pushing back. That's how we do business.

Get me that bid card and I'll review it. Once I see it, we're good. I'll reach out to Oscar today on the credit piece so he's not blindsided.

Talk in a second.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Appreciate that, Sarah. You're getting it now — check it over and you'll see everything laid out. Title's clean, variance is documented, full option list right there.

Oscar's gonna be expecting your call today. You're all set.

Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Yeah, I got a minute. What've you got for me today?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, loaded with the Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro — all the good stuff. Black Sapphire, beautiful condition. Heads up though — there's an odometer variance on the Carfax. The auction reported fifty-nine eight, but the prior Carfax showed fifty-five two. I wanted to lead with that since it's the headline.

Otherwise clean title, no accidents. We're targeting thirty-one five on this one. MMR's sitting at twenty-seven, so we're in a fair spot. Could this work for your lot?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, I appreciate you leading with the odometer thing — that's the right move. Look, that's a red flag I gotta factor in, even if the rest of the car is clean. Buyers see that and they're gonna ask questions, and I'm gonna have to answer 'em.

Here's the thing — that's a nice spec, no question. But thirty-one five is high for me with that variance hanging over it. I want it, but you gotta come down.

What's your bottom line here? And be real with me — where can you actually go on price?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you. That's fair — the variance does complicate the retail story, and you're right that your customer's gonna zero in on it.

Here's where I'm at: thirty-one five is already solid value against rBook — we're nine grand under. But I get that the odometer flag changes your math on what you can retail it for. 

What number gets you comfortable? Give me a real ask and let's see if we can make it work.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright, I respect that you're not dancing around it.

Look, with that odometer variance, I'm looking at a retail hit. I can't put this on my lot at full price — I gotta absorb that risk, and my customers are gonna want a discount because of it. That cuts into my margin.

I can do twenty-eight K. That's real money for you, and it gives me room to work it retail without getting buried.

I know that's a step down, but that's where my math works with the variance in play. Can you meet me somewhere in that zone, or do you need to think about it?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I get the math — you're being straight with me. Twenty-eight is a solid move on your end, but that's a three-five drop from where I'm sitting, and that's too big a jump for me to eat in one go.

Here's what I can do: Thirty K. That's five hundred under my ask, and it gives you real room to work the retail story without me taking all the variance hit. You pick it up at thirty K, and the car moves fast because the spec is there — Executive, Driving Assist Pro, sixty K miles.

That close enough to your number?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Thirty K... that's better, but you're still asking me to carry too much of that variance risk.

Here's the thing — the car's solid, the spec is there, I get it. But I gotta move it retail, and that odometer flag is gonna be a conversation with every buyer who walks around it. That's friction.

I can do twenty-nine two. That's real movement toward you — I'm only a couple hundred off thirty — but it gives me breathing room on the retail side. The spec helps, yeah, but I need something in my pocket for the work I'm gonna do explaining that Carfax.

Can you do twenty-nine two and call it a deal? Or do you need me to sweeten it another way?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Twenty-nine two is close. I can feel where you're coming from on the retail friction — that's real.

Here's what I can do: Twenty-nine five. That splits the difference, and I'll throw in priority delivery — we can have it on your lot by end of week if you need it faster. That gives you time to get it prepped and on the floor without sitting on it.

Twenty-nine five and we're locked in. Does that work?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Twenty-nine five with priority delivery end of week...

Yeah, okay. I can work with that. That gives me enough margin, and honestly, getting it on the lot fast is helpful — I can get ahead of the retail story if it's here quick. Less time sitting around, more time selling.

You got yourself a deal, Bill. Twenty-nine five, locked in, end of week delivery. Send me the paperwork and let's get this thing moving.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Watch for that text — you'll see the full card, photos, the odometer flag right up top, everything. One of the owners will follow up with you tomorrow to lock in the delivery timeline and get the paperwork rolling.

Good talking to you, Sarah. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill, yeah I've got a few minutes. What's on your mind?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Great. So we just caught a twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, black sapphire, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. We're sitting at thirty-one five on it.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
A twenty-one 750i, that's a nice unit. Sapphire black with all that – yeah, I can see the appeal. But thirty-one five? That's high, Bill. I mean, I want it, but you gotta come down from there.

What's your bottom line on this? I'm thinking I can do twenty-eight K, maybe twenty-eight-five if the service records are clean. That's where I need to be to make this work for my customers.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you — twenty-eight is definitely aggressive, but let me be straight with you. We're already nine grand under rBook on this one. MMR's at twenty-seven, so we're only four-five above that comp number. 

Here's the thing though — there's an odometer variance on it. Bid showed fifty-nine-eight, but Carfax pulled fifty-five-two-eight-three. I wanted to lead with that because it matters. Could knock some value off, but it's already reflected in where we're sitting at thirty-one five.

What if we move to thirty? That's solid room for your customer base and still leaves us reasonable. Can you work with thirty?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Okay, I appreciate you being straight with me on that odometer thing – that's the kind of detail that matters. But Bill, that's also exactly why I can't go to thirty. That variance is a red flag for my customers, even if it's explained. They're gonna ask questions, and I'm gonna have to discount it harder on the back end anyway.

Look, the comp data is what it is, but I'm the one holding the bag if there's any blowback on that mileage discrepancy. That's risk on my end.

Here's where I'm at: I can do twenty-eight-five, and that's my number. But if you want to sweeten it – what if you knock out a full pre-purchase inspection on your dime? Or better yet, give me some dealer credit I can use on future buys? That way I'm not eating the whole risk on the odometer thing and you're showing good faith.

Can you work with that?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You're not wrong — that variance does add friction for retail, I get it. And I appreciate you naming the number straight.

Twenty-eight-five plus a pre-purchase on us is actually reasonable. But here's what I can do: I can get you to twenty-nine flat, and we throw the inspection. That splits the difference and shows we stand behind it. Plus you're protected going into retail.

On the dealer credit angle — that's something Oscar would need to lock in since it rolls into the relationship book. But I'm confident he'd want to do something there given the volume we've built with you.

Does twenty-nine plus the inspection work, and I'll have Oscar circle back on the credit piece?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*pauses*

Twenty-nine flat plus the inspection... I mean, that's better. And I appreciate Oscar being willing to talk credit – that matters to me.

Here's the thing though, Bill – you're still asking me to eat five hundred on top of the comp to account for that odometer variance. The inspection helps, but it doesn't change what my customer sees on the Carfax report.

Look, I want to make this work. We've got a good thing going, and I don't want to walk away from a solid unit. But I need twenty-eight-five, the inspection, and I need you to get Oscar on the phone this week to lock in something concrete on dealer credit – not a maybe, an actual number we can put in writing for my next deal.

Can you do that? Get me that commitment from Oscar on the credit side, and we've got a deal today.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you, Sarah. You're right — you're taking on the retail risk here, and that's real.

Let me be honest: I can get you to twenty-eight-five plus the inspection. That's solid and it's fair. But on the dealer credit commitment — I want to commit to that for you, but Oscar's the one who structures those terms, and he needs to own it so it's locked in properly and not just a verbal.

Here's what I'm gonna do right now: I'm gonna text you the full bid card while we're on the phone. Then I'm calling Oscar the second we hang up and telling him you need a concrete credit number this week — not a maybe. He'll ring you back by end of day tomorrow with an actual figure in writing.

Deal at twenty-eight-five, inspection on us, and Oscar commits the credit in writing by EOD tomorrow. Can we make that work?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*takes a breath*
